title: Senior Director of Payments Solutions
raw_jd:
About the job

The Senior Director of Payments Solutions is a highly visible and mission-critical role that will lead the technical transformation of Aledade's Practice Payments operations. Reporting to the VP of Enterprise Applications, this role will oversee the technical team and solutions architecture required to scale our payment operations from current levels (24+ payment types) to over $2B in annual Practice Payments within 2-3 years. This position is essential to Aledade's ability to fulfill our contractual payment obligations to healthcare practice partners and will directly impact our operational success and growth trajectory.

We are looking for a player-coach who is hands-on and is able to scale across strategic topics as well as get into the details and work alongside their team. The Sr. Director must be highly collaborative and effective at working across business functions and organizational boundaries. Success in this role is highly dependent on the ability to be collaborative and effectively influence across the organization.

Primary Duties:

    Technical Solutions Leadership
    Lead the Payment Solutions technical team (Business Analyst, 2 System Administrators to start, additional team growth is expected) in designing and implementing scalable payment solutions
    Oversee current payment technology stack and drive optimization of existing solutions (QuickCap, NetSuite, etc.)
    Spearhead the evaluation and implementation of a second payment solution to handle non-claims based payment types
    Ensure payment systems architecture can scale to support $2B+ in annual payment volume
    Drive automation initiatives to eliminate manual processes between market leaders, central business owners, Payments and Finance teams
    Team Building & Leadership
    Manage and develop initial team of 3 direct reports (1 Business Analyst, 2 System Administrators)
    Build strategic hiring plan and organizational structure to scale the Payments team in alignment with business growth
    Define roles, responsibilities, and career progression paths for expanding payment operations team
    Foster a culture of technical excellence, continuous improvement, and operational reliability
    Establish team processes and methodologies to support rapid scaling while maintaining quality
    Process Architecture & Standardization
    Design standardized, scalable workflows for all 24+ payment types currently managed
    Develop technical specifications and requirements for payment processing improvements
    Create systematic approaches to bank reconciliation processes to address current pain points
    Establish playbook (including technical guardrails, approval workflows and org-wide implications) to facilitate standing up new payment types
    Cross-Functional Partnership & Governance
    Partner with Security team to ensure payment solutions meet cybersecurity and data protection standards
    Collaborate with CRG (Compliance, Risk, Governance) team on regulatory requirements and governance frameworks
    Work closely with Growth, Provider Networks, and Finance teams to ensure streamlined handoffs for full end-to-end processes, in compliance with audit and SOX requirements
    Support Growth and Provider Networks teams by defining technical feasibility parameters for new payment options during contract negotiations
    Work with Engineering and Analytics teams to ensure compliance with organizational data architecture standards
    Systems Integration & Management
    Oversee integration between payment solutions and existing enterprise applications
    Ensure seamless data flow between payment systems, financial systems, and reporting platforms
    Standardize the user experience across the various payment systems and processes
    Manage vendor relationships for payment technology solutions
    Drive system performance monitoring and optimization initiatives
    Strategic Planning & Execution
    Develop technical roadmap for payment solutions that aligns with business growth projections
    Create scalability assessments and capacity planning for payment infrastructure
    Establish technical KPIs and metrics for payment system performance and reliability
    Support enterprise-wide Payments Excellence program with technical expertise and solutions

Minimum Qualifications:

    Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Finance, or related technical field; advanced degree preferred
    12+ years of experience managing payments technology, financial systems, or enterprise applications
    3-5 years of experience implementing payment systems, processes, and related technology and support adoption by working with business partners and stakeholders
    5+ years of management experience leading technical teams, with demonstrated ability to scale organizations
    Extensive hands-on experience with payment processing systems and financial technology platforms
    Proven track record scaling payment operations in high-growth environments
    Strong understanding of payment reconciliation, financial controls, and audit requirements
    Experience with enterprise system integrations and data management
    Demonstrated ability to manage complex, high-visibility technical initiatives
    Experience building and scaling technical teams from startup through enterprise levels

Preferred Knowledge, Skills and/or Abilities:

    Experience with QuickCap, NetSuite, or similar enterprise payment/ERP solutions
    Ideal candidate will have deep healthcare industry expertise, particularly in value-based care models and payor contract structures, to ensure our practice payment solutions meet all specialized accounting requirements
    Background in financial services or fintech environments
    Experience with API integrations and payment gateway technologies
    Knowledge of banking systems, ACH processing, and financial reconciliation platforms
    Project management certification (PMP) or similar credentials
    Strong communication skills for presenting to executive-level stakeholders

Physical Requirements:

    Sitting for prolonged periods of time. Extensive use of computers and keyboard. Occasional walking and lifting may be required.

Who We Are:

Aledade, a public benefit corporation, exists to empower the most transformational part of our health care landscape - independent primary care. We were founded in 2014, and since then, we've become the largest network of independent primary care in the country - helping practices, health centers and clinics deliver better care to their patients and thrive in value-based care. Additionally, by creating value-based contracts across a wide variety of health plans, we aim to flip the script on the traditional fee-for-service model. Our work strengthens continuity of care, aligns incentives and ensures primary care physicians are paid for what they do best - keeping patients healthy. If you want to help create a health care system that is good for patients, good for practices and good for society - and if you're eager to join a collaborative, inclusive and remote-first culture - you've come to the right place.

What Does This Mean for You?

At Aledade, you will be part of a creative culture that is driven by a passion for tackling complex issues with respect, open-mindedness and a desire to learn. You will collaborate with team members who bring a wide range of experiences, interests, backgrounds, beliefs and achievements to their work - and who are all united by a shared passion for public health and a commitment to the Aledade mission.

In addition to time off to support work-life balance and enjoyment, we offer the following comprehensive benefits package designed for the overall well-being of our team members:

Flexible work schedules and the ability to work remotely are available for many roles

Health, dental and vision insurance paid up to 80% for employees, dependents and domestic partners

Robust time-off plan (21 days of PTO in your first year)

Two paid volunteer days and 11 paid holidays

12 weeks paid parental leave for all new parents

Six weeks paid sabbatical after six years of service

Educational Assistant Program and Clinical Employee Reimbursement Program

401(k) with up to 4% match

Stock options

And much more!

At Aledade, we don’t just accept differences, we celebrate them! We strive to attract, develop and retain highly qualified individuals representing the diverse communities where we live and work. Aledade is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Employment policies and decisions at Aledade are based on merit, qualifications, performance and business needs. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth or medical conditions related to pregnancy or childbirth), gender identity or expression, religion, physical or mental disability, medical condition, legally protected genetic information, marital status, veteran status, or sexual orientation.

Privacy Policy: By applying for this job, you agree to Aledade's Applicant Privacy Policy available at https://www.aledade.com/privacy-policy-applicants

We may use automated tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to help organize and evaluate application materials. These tools support our recruiters and hiring managers by helping manage large applicant pools. Human judgment plays an essential role in our hiring process, including in the oversight and use of any automated tools. If you would like more information about our screening and hiring process, please contact us.

Benefits found in job post

Vision insurance, 401(k)

score.skills_rationale: This is primarily a payments and financial systems leadership role requiring deep healthcare payments expertise. Candidate's background is in data analytics, BI, and ERP reporting - some overlap with financial systems but missing core payments processing, reconciliation, and healthcare payments domain knowledge.
score.scope_rationale: The scope perfectly matches candidate's senior leadership level - managing technical teams, scaling operations, cross-functional collaboration, and strategic planning. The 'player-coach' expectation aligns with candidate's hands-on technical leadership background.
score.pay_rationale: No salary range provided in JD. Senior Director level at a well-funded healthcare company would likely exceed candidate's W2 target range significantly.
score.raw_analysis: This is a highly specialized payments and financial systems leadership role in healthcare that requires domain expertise the candidate lacks. While the leadership scope and technical management aspects align well with the candidate's background, the core requirement for payments processing and healthcare finance expertise creates a significant skills gap. The role is likely well-compensated but represents a career pivot rather than a natural progression.
